preamble: Debuccalization of the voiceless aspirated dental stop [tʰ] into the glottal fricative [h] represents a significant weakening, although it is less common for dental stops than for labial or velar stops.

examples.note: Historical developments in various language families where aspiration is the only surviving feature of former stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
